title: Is Armisael 4D and Introduction
wikitext:
[]: Is Armisael 4D & Introduction?             Quote Post by TheAyanamiOtaku » Sat May 10, 2008 3:30 am Okay after reading the Wikipedia entry for our dear Armie and looking back on her introduction, Armisael pops up unexpectedly without arriving from the Sea or somewhere else, similar to Leliel's arrival. Four-dimensional beings are said to have three dimensional shadows...which is the case for Leliel...unless the pattern is not a tattooed sphere but a two-dimensional object in the sky...and they are also believed to have the ability to literally see through you, like how we can see words on a piece of paper, so that might explain her attitude with Rei and using her emotions she feels against her. anyways So Armisael shows up in teh middle of the forest, without any warning, and Misato orders the Evas to "ok let's go" and Misato From Ep.23 wrote: Launch Eva 00 from the 32nd Gate. Set Unit 02 as backup

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat May 10, 2008 5:14 am I'm not sure Misato's line actually means she's questioning whether or not Armisael is "in the corporeal realm". While I'm honestly not sure offhand why she says it, I just figured it was innocuous and maybe reflecting on the fact that another one's showed up. I dunno lol. Other Angels have mysteriously "appeared out of nowhere", such as Zeruel, as well.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Sat May 10, 2008 5:55 am Pretty much the same for Ramiel. And let's not forget Arael and Sahaquiel suddenly appearing in orbit. Why does Misato say that? Maybe after seeing an entire procession of weirdo creatures come and go, she doesn't know if that's an entire Angel she's seeing or just part of it. Leliel would be to blame, of course.

[]:             Quote Post by sadsadshinji » Sat May 10, 2008 9:51 am I guess on top of the confusion Leliel caused, Armisael was cycling between blood pattern orange and blue, but it seems like Misato didnt know that when she commented "Angel visually confirmed...right?" I was also under the impression that Leliel wasnt so much a fourth dimensional creature as a creature that could alter time-space and create the "Sea of Dirac". I suppose that can be considered a fourth dimensional ability though.

[]:             Quote Post by Dartz » Sat May 10, 2008 10:08 am But if I drive straight towards that wall, I'm going to hit those Indians, right? Marty, Marty Marty, you're not thinking 4th dimensionally. when the DeLorean hits 88mph you will be instantly transported back to 1885 and those indians won't even exist...

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Sat May 10, 2008 10:22 am Dartz wrote: But if I drive straight towards that wall, I'm going to hit those Indians, right? Marty, Marty Marty, you're not thinking 4th dimensionally. when the DeLorean hits 88mph you will be instantly transported back to 1885 and those indians won't even exist...

[]:             Quote Post by TheAyanamiOtaku » Sat May 10, 2008 2:23 pm I loled. But back on topic female canines?/color_abusing

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sun May 11, 2008 12:01 am @Back to the Future: Nice. Misato asking that could imply there's OTHER MONSTERS BESIDES THE ANGELS!!!! :wut: The sea of Dirac may be a plane directly accessible by the Angels, seeing that Leliel is a portal to there, and Shamshel's core had the potential to create one (if assuming that when Unit-04 "disappeared" it went into a sea of Dirac using the modified S2-Organ, otherwise known as Shamshel's core), it wouldn't be surprising if more than those Angel's used the sea to magically "appear" out of nowhere, such as with Ramiel, Leliel, and Armisael. (Heck, even Bardiel if we put enough thought into it). To put that jumble of words into a fashion that makes sense. The sea is something the Angels can manifest and manipulate whenever they want. Maybe.

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Sun May 11, 2008 12:27 am F-F-F-F-F-FANWANK!

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Sun May 11, 2008 12:29 am Angels popping out of plotholes? Topic derailment alert!

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon May 12, 2008 12:56 am I think the Dirac Sea fanwank is a really ancient one. It might still be on Eva Sage's site somewhere. Either that, or Archive.org.

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Mon May 12, 2008 9:46 am Reichu wrote: It might still be on Eva Sage's site somewhere.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon May 12, 2008 11:29 am One thing I've been uncertain about is whether or not the Dirac Sea is really THE Dirac Sea, or, in Leliel's case, we were dealing with a Dirac Sea. Ritsuko said something like Leliel's inverted A.T. Field contained an imaginary space (= Dirac Sea), so... if Leliel went bye-bye, wouldn't the imaginary space contained by her A.T. Field? Ritsuko did allude to the Dirac Sea connecting to some other place, though. (LCL Sea, anyone?) In any case, the intimation that Leliel had her own Dirac Sea that was subsequently destroyed has made me terribly confused when folks fanwank that there's a common Dirac Sea that all the Angels can access to do weird teleportation tricks and the like. And was the Dirac Sea ever actually held directly responsible for the fact that Leliel "appeared out of nowhere" in the first place? I mean, Leliel can use it to make other things disappear into "nowhere", but... How does one pop in and out of the "imaginary space" contained by one's own A.T. Field? I think it's simpler if Angels just have ATF Cloaking Technology that they can use until the script wants them to be detected, personally.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon May 12, 2008 6:47 pm Reichu wrote: How does one pop in and out of the "imaginary space" contained by one's own A.T. Field?

[]:             Quote Post by universalperson » Mon May 12, 2008 8:20 pm I get the impression that a Dirac Sea is a bad way to describe this. Probably a better explination would be a world between worlds, or a shadow of reality.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon May 12, 2008 9:18 pm @Sachi: By "imaginary", I believe Ritsuko meant in the mathematical sense. Unless you were, too.

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Tue May 13, 2008 9:12 am Reichu wrote: One thing I've been uncertain about is whether or not the Dirac Sea is really THE Dirac Sea, or, in Leliel's case, we were dealing with a Dirac Sea.

[]:             Quote Post by Dartz » Tue May 13, 2008 12:50 pm I thought a Dirac sea was a theoretical model of a vacuum, made up of below zero energy particles that statistically on average, do not exist. Or something like that. It's all a load of quantum hairyness. In this usage, it's just technobabble... high quality technobabble, but still just technobable. It probably means little or nothing. Then again, given the fact that Unit 04 tore open a Dirac sea when it's S^2 engine went pop, it would seem that the general intention is for vacuum energy to be some sort of power source for the Angels. While vacuum energy is known to exist, harnessing it usefully is a different kettle of fish. Only thing it's done so far is produce a tiny force between two metal plates that are really close to each other. Vacuum energy might be behind the Cosmological constant Which isn't really 4th dimensional. It's Quantum. Therefore epson factory, Angels are not 4th dimensional beings, they're just fugitives from the second law of thermodynamics sucking up free energy.... which is only a law by statistics anyway. Which also means I need some Paracetemol.

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Tue May 13, 2008 4:01 pm Dartz wrote: Then again, given the fact that Unit 04 tore open a Dirac sea when it's S^2 engine went pop, it would seem that the general intention is for vacuum energy to be some sort of power source for the Angels. While vacuum energy is known to exist, harnessing it usefully is a different kettle of fish.

[]:             Quote Post by evilhead008 » Tue May 20, 2008 6:15 pm But does NGE follow quantum mechanics? Most of the time when something is physically impossible (generally the weight and heigh impossibillities), the response is "The ATF makes it possible." Which isn't really 4th dimensional. It's Quantum. Therefore epson factory, Angels are not 4th dimensional beings, they're just fugitives from the second law of thermodynamics sucking up free energy.... which is only a law by statistics anyway.
